shortDesc.message: Check the open URL with SEO Score Checker: SEO score, SERP preview, meta tags, Traffic Risk, and top fixes.

storeDesc.message:
🔎 SEO Score Checker gives a fast 0–100 rating for the active tab in Chrome. It is made for manual page-level review: open a tab, click the toolbar icon, and see the on-page signals that deserve attention first.

Use it before publishing a landing page, updating an article, reviewing a product page, or checking client work during a call.

⚡ What you see

• 0–100 result for the current URL
• Grade: Good, Needs improvement, or Critical issues
• Traffic Risk: Low, Medium, or High
• Top 3 fixes with evidence, reason, suggested change, and confidence
• SERP preview based on the detected title and meta description
• Breakdown for indexability, metadata, headings, schema, images, links, and technical basics

🧠 Why the result is explainable

The extension does more than show raw tags. For each high-priority item, it explains:

1️⃣ what was detected
2️⃣ why it may affect search visibility or clicks
3️⃣ what to change next
4️⃣ how confident the check is

The rating focuses on high-confidence HTML signals. Social tags, language, charset, and link counts are shown as secondary insights, so they do not outweigh problems like noindex, a missing title, a bad canonical, or no H1.

📊 Traffic Risk

Traffic Risk is a priority label, not a revenue estimate, ROI forecast, or money-loss calculation. It helps you decide which issue to review first.

Examples:

• High: noindex, missing title, broken canonical, or missing H1
• Medium: missing meta description, weak heading structure, or many images without alt text
• Low: secondary metadata or minor supporting signals

🧩 Metadata and SERP preview

Use the extension to check SEO score and inspect the title, meta description, canonical URL, and robots meta directives for the open tab. The SERP preview tool helps you see whether the search snippet looks clear before publishing.

It can work as a simple meta tag checker, title tag checker, and meta description checker without opening several separate tools.

🚦 Indexability and structure

The report reviews common on-page SEO signals, including:

➤ noindex directives
➤ canonical settings
➤ title and description presence
➤ H1 usage
➤ heading structure
➤ viewport basics

This is useful when you need an on page SEO checker for a fast manual review, not a crawler.

🧱 Schema, images, and links

The extension also shows supporting checks:

• JSON-LD and structured data presence
• schema checker and structured data summary
• images without alt text
• internal and external link counts
• basic technical signals

These insights help explain the result, but they stay secondary in the final priority.

👥 Who it helps

This tool is useful for marketers, founders, content teams, consultants, and site owners. Use it when you want to check your SEO score before publishing or after edits.

You can also use it to:

1. review a landing URL before launch
2. inspect metadata after content changes
3. check page SEO score for a client URL
4. check website SEO score one URL at a time
5. find practical next steps instead of only raw tags

🔒 Privacy and local analysis

The core analysis runs in your browser on the currently open tab. The extension is designed without login, crawler, or external API calls for the main review.

📌 What it does not do

The extension reviews the current page, not an entire domain.

It is not:

• a full website SEO audit
• a domain crawler
• a rank tracker
• a backlink database
• a keyword research platform
• a PageSpeed or Core Web Vitals platform

For large sitewide audits, exports, crawl reports, backlink data, or ranking history, use a dedicated SEO audit tool.

🙋 FAQ

1) Is this a full domain analyzer?

No. It is a URL-level tool. You can check website SEO score manually across several URLs, but it does not crawl a whole domain.

2) Can I use it for a quick page SEO audit?

Yes. It helps with a quick manual review before deeper analysis in larger platforms.

3) Does the priority label predict revenue?

No. It is only a heuristic label for organic visibility, snippets, clicks, and common publishing issues.

4) Why did the URL get this rating?

The popup connects the result to findings. Important recommendations include evidence, why the issue matters, what to change, and confidence.

✅ Install SEO Score Checker to review the open URL, preview its search snippet, inspect key on-page signals, and find clear top fixes in one click.
